---
title: "Contributing"
---

**We welcome PRs to this repo from anyone. If you open a PR with new or revised content, please add yourself to the authors list in `index.qmd`.**

Once a PR is approved, the PR author should merge it.

This site uses [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) to generate website pages using markdown and jupyter notebooks.

## Adding pages

To add a new page:

1. Create new `.qmd` or `.ipynb` file
2. Add the new page to the `sidebar` section in `_quarto.yml` so it shows up in the sidebar navigation

## Previews

To preview changes, you can run this site locally (see instructions to setup quarto locally below) or use the automated preview generation when opening a PR (see [the PR preview workflow](./.github/workflows/pr-preview.yml)).

## Release Process

Releases mark citable snapshots of the report. Not every merged PR needs a release — cut one when there is a meaningful body of new or revised content.

### Steps to create a release

1. **Decide on a version number.** Use `vMAJOR.MINOR` (e.g., `v1.0`, `v1.1`, `v2.0`). Increment the major version for significant restructuring or scope changes; increment the minor version for new sections or substantial updates.

2. **Update `releases.md`.** Add an entry at the top of the list with the version, date, and a short summary of what changed.

3. **Open and merge a PR** with the `releases.md` update. The site will be published automatically on merge to `main`.

4. **Create a GitHub Release.** Go to [Releases → Draft a new release](https://github.com/NASA-IMPACT/virtual-stores-feasibility-report/releases/new):
   - Set the tag to match the version (e.g., `v1.1`), targeting the `main` branch.
   - Use the version as the title (e.g., `v1.1`).
   - Copy the summary from `releases.md` into the release notes.
   - Publish the release.

## Local Development

### Install Quarto

Install Quarto by downloading the installer for your platform from [quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/), or via a package manager:

- **macOS (Homebrew):** `brew install --cask quarto`
- **Linux:** Download the `.deb` or `.rpm` package from the Quarto website
- **Windows:** Download the `.msi` installer from the Quarto website

Verify the installation with `quarto check`.

### Run quarto commands

To preview the site locally:

```bash
quarto preview
```

This runs `quarto preview`, which serves the site and reloads on changes.
